source_pr.title: executor: fix unstable test TestStaleReadPrepare
source_pr.body:

### What is changed and how it works?

symptoms: TestStaleReadPrepare was timing-sensitive around capturing an AS OF timestamp and then performing an update; small timing differences could make the stale-read assertions unstable (executor/stale_txn_test.go).
failure_signature: Stale-read prepare test intermittently fails due to timestamp boundary/timing sensitivity (executor/stale_txn_test.go).
root_cause_explanation: The test captures a timestamp variable `@a` and then asserts stale-read behavior (`AS OF TIMESTAMP @a`) while concurrent updates happen. This is timing-sensitive: if the captured timestamp and subsequent writes are too close, the test can observe unexpected values. The fix stabilizes the sequence by capturing `@a` from `@@tidb_current_ts` via `tidb_parse_tso(...)` inside a transaction and using small sleeps to ensure a clear timestamp boundary before the update and assertions (executor/stale_txn_test.go).
fix_pattern: Stabilize stale-read timestamp capture/ordering (use `tidb_parse_tso(@@tidb_current_ts)` in a txn) and add minimal sleeps to ensure the update happens after the captured timestamp.

evidence: evidence: executor: fix unstable test TestStaleReadPrepare
